chapterTitle: Chapter 2: HTML Document Structure & Writing Clean Code

options.text: Changes the page color to English theme
Specifies the language of the page content

options.text: As a heading on the page

MCQs.question: What character encoding should you always use?

ShortQuestions.question: What is the purpose of <!DOCTYPE html> and what happens if you skip it?
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: `<!DOCTYPE html>` tells the browser that the document is written in HTML5 and should be rendered using modern standards. If you skip it, the browser enters **Quirks Mode**, where it guesses how to render the page. This can cause inconsistent layouts and unexpected behavior across different browsers.

ShortQuestions.question: What is the difference between <head> and <body>?
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: The `<head>` section contains **metadata** — information about the page that is NOT displayed on the screen (like the title, character encoding, and description). The `<body>` section contains all the **visible content** that users see and interact with (headings, paragraphs, images, links, etc.).

ShortQuestions.question: Why is the lang attribute important on the <html> tag? Give two reasons.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
1. **Accessibility:** Screen readers use the `lang` attribute to pronounce words in the correct language and accent, helping visually impaired users.
2. **SEO:** Search engines like Google use the `lang` attribute to understand the content language and show the page to the right audience in search results.

ShortQuestions.question: Write the correct nesting for a paragraph containing bold and italic text that says "HTML is amazing".
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
```html

HTML is amazing

``` The rule is **last opened = first closed**. `` was opened last, so it closes first. Then `` closes. Finally `

` closes.

ShortQuestions.question: List all the essential tags/elements that every proper HTML5 page must have.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
Every proper HTML5 page must have:
1. `<!DOCTYPE html>` — HTML5 declaration
2. `<html lang="en">` — Root element with language
3. `<head>` — Metadata section
4. `<meta charset="UTF-8">` — Character encoding
5. `<meta name="viewport">` — Mobile responsiveness
6. `<title>` — Browser tab title
7. `<body>` — Visible content container
